LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BLAKE SANFORD'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT AS TO THE EIGHTH AND NINTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR FRAUD AND NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFFS EDDY AND JENNIFER VATARU IS OVERRULED AS TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE VATARUS HAVE ADEQUATELY ALLEGED CLAIMS FOR INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION FRAUD AND NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION. THE VATARUS ALLEGED A MISREPRESENTATION AS TO AN EXISTING FACT: THE STATUS OF MR. SANFORD'S CONTRACTOR'S LICENSE AT THE TIME THE VATARUS ENTERED INTO A CONTRACT TO HIRE MR. SANFORD AS THE GENERAL CONTRACTOR. (FAC PARS. 72-78; 79-82). THE VATARUS ALLEGE THAT, HAD THEY KNOWN MR. SANFORD HAD A RESTRICTED CONTRACTOR'S LICENSE, THEY WOULD NOT HAVE ENTERED INTO THE CONTRACT WITH MR. SANFORD. THESE ALLEGATIONS DO NOT REST ON FUTURE CONDUCT, BUT FOCUS ON THE ASSERTION THAT MR. SANFORD HAD A RESTRICTED LICENSE AT THE TIME THE VATARUS DECIDED TO HIRE HIM. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED

DEFENDANT VINCENT LEGER, AND EAG STUDIO'S Renewed Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement.

Defendants Vincent Leger and EAG Studio's renewed motion for determination of good faith settlement is granted. The settlement between plaintiffs and the EAG defendants is determined to be in good faith per CCP 877.6 and the EAG defendants are entitled to all rights resulting from that determination. The court refrains from allocating the settlement funds and will leave that issue to be resolved by the trial judge. No party objects to the good faith determination. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-12-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Matt Haulk, Esq. (415-453-9433) of Ragghianti & Freitas LLP for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT BLAKE SANFORD'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT as to the eighth and ninth causes of action for fraud and negligent misrepresentation in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiffs Eddy and Jennifer Vataru is OVERRULED as to both causes of action. The Vatarus have adequately alleged claims for intentional misrepresentation fraud and negligent misrepresentation. The Vatarus alleged a misrepresentation as to an existing fact: the status of Mr. Sanford's contractor's license at the time the Vatarus entered into a contract to hire Mr. Sanford as the general contractor. (FAC pars. 72-78; 79-82). The Vatarus allege that, had they known Mr. Sanford had a restricted contractor's license, they would not have entered into the contract with Mr. Sanford. These allegations do not rest on future conduct, but focus on the assertion that Mr. Sanford had a restricted license at the time the Vatarus decided to hire him. Order is signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. =(302/hk)
